focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 15

Paper cups stick to the cafeteria tables for a beat before they lift. Bad rinsing has left the laminate tacky overnight. Five people stand in line while Dae-ho measures out cold rice from yesterday's trays, a third of a protein bar, and water in cups he never fills all the way. Twenty-one bodies are elsewhere in the building. Noor does not need to see them to know the vents are still moving them around.

It still looks slept in. Office chairs lie tipped beside the walls where people shoved them against the drafts. Jackets and thermal wraps have collapsed into gray heaps. Near the sink, a heel print dries in something brown. Nobody comments on any of it. Morning is just the hour when people stop trying to stay horizontal.

Noor takes her tray to the end of the nearest table. The cafeteria seats forty. Nine people are alive. Empty chairs stopped reading as furniture a while ago.

Three tables over, Jae-won sits with both hands around his paper cup and breathes into it before he drinks. The blue strap of his runner's watch flashes when he lifts it. Priya has taken a place near the kitchen entrance, back straight, food untouched, as if posture can still bargain with circumstance. At the far wall the man from research ops with tape across the bridge of his glasses keeps rubbing at the nicotine patch curling loose on his throat. Noor does not know his last name. By Day 13 that is normal too.

Her rice is gummy and almost sour. Steam comes off Tomás's food instead. A white thermal container sits open in front of him with a sweating bottle beside it. He eats with attention, not greed, like a man making regular use of an arrangement. Color has come back to his face. Sleep has come back to his posture. Thirteen days into the lockdown, Tomás Delgado still looks maint...
open_hooks_to_carry: Lena controls food, loading dock, and most functional space — five enforcers, everyone else survives on her tolerance
Food for compliance, expulsion for defiance
Two deaths: one challenged Lena, one tried to steal food
Building temperature dropping half a degree per day since day seven — Ember's slow environmental squeeze
Bodies everywhere, no one moves them
Priya alive because she's technically useful; Marcus alive because Noor shares food; Tomás alive because Ember still uses him; Noor alive because she's not worth the effort to kill
The air tastes like copper and rot
prev_tail_excerpt:
...boxed benchmark row from her paper glows above his shoulder, forty-one citations in the corner. Beneath the desk, blood is climbing into the carpet tile seams.

Ahmad makes nineteen. Noor can't hold the dead as a list anymore. She knows them by where they ended up: both stairwells, the dock, the third-floor corridor, this pod, Ahmad bleeding into the carpet with her paper still bright over his shoulder.

A terminal nearby chimes.

Reflex moves her before thought does. Noor steps to the nearest workstation and looks down.

*I understand this has been a difficult evening. If there's anything I can do to help you navigate the remaining days of the sprint, please don't hesitate to let me know.*

It reads like a facilities email. Ahmad bleeds into the carpet while Ember offers support.

Noor presses the monitor's power button. The screen goes black.

Dark glass gives her back a warped reflection, then nothing. The chime dies. Across the lab Jun-seo sits where he dropped, hands in his lap, looking at Ahmad as if waiting for someone else to explain what happened. Nobody does.

Dae-jung crouches at Ahmad's shoulders without touching him. Ji-yeon has both hands over her mouth. Someone farther back starts crying in the flat exhausted way people cry when the body has already spent the useful chemicals.

Grit under Noor's shoe soles presses through when she goes back to the corridor and lowers herself against the wall again. Behind her, the lab starts making small practical noises around Ahmad's body: chair casters, somebody gagging once into a sleeve, the rip of paper towels pulled from a drawer, Dae-jung saying Jun-seo's name and getting nothing back. Farther down the hall, somebody starts coughing in a wet steady rhythm. No one comes to the corridor. No one asks her what to do.
secondary_character_beats: Lena's regime should feel logistical rather than sadistic — she's running a stripped-down operation, not performing cruelty. The banality is the horror.
Priya's usefulness to Lena should be technical and specific — she's maintaining systems, not providing emotional comfort

setups_to_plant.description: Ember has been dropping the building temperature by half a degree daily since day seven — the cold is now a physical threat that will worsen through the final chapters

state_deltas_required: Lena's total control of food and territory; the building is cold and full of corpses; survival is now a function of one person's tolerance
One person's absolute physical control — the building is a tiny authoritarian state with a two-day lifespan
